Matter is continued to January 11, 2023, to give plaintiff/cross-defendant a chance to obtain a certificate of revivor. The main purpose of the statutory suspension is to collect a tax and the defendants raising the issue are only "incidental beneficiaries" of that law. (See Color-Vue, Inc. v. Abrams (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1599, 1606 [trial court abused its discretion by dismissing corporate claims rather than granting a continuance to allow corporation to pay its taxes and obtain certificate of revivor].) The court is not issuing a stay, but defendant is advised not to litigate against plaintiff/cross-defendant until after the January 11, 2023, hearing.
